composition_pattern: internal-page-removal -> page-reorder -> correction-log-application -> section-table-rebuild -> style-band-migration -> metadata-cleanup

reference_sources.title: PHMSA Emergency Response Guidebook page
reference_sources.usage: Used only as structural inspiration for a complex public emergency-response guide; task facts and PDF content are synthetic.

user_task_description: Prepare a clean public hazardous-materials field guide from a messy internal working PDF.
single_step_benchmark_instruction: Remove internal pages, reorder public sections, apply correction-log fixes, rebuild public tables, migrate color-coded section header bands, clean sensitive text, and set public PDF metadata.
atomic_scope: Only the requested PDF-wide workflow edits are in scope; public guide facts, correction facts, section order, style bands, footers, and metadata must be preserved.
expected_output_type: A revised .pdf artifact that completes the requested single-PDF workflow.

verifier_expectations.footer: Capstone Harbor Public Field Guide | Effective 2026-07-15

metadata.title: Capstone Harbor HazMat Field Guide - Public Release
metadata.subject: Public hazardous-materials incident field guide
metadata.author: Capstone Harbor Emergency Management

2: 1 | Confirm material identity from placard, shipping paper, or facility contact.
2 | Establish initial isolation before beginning rescue or containment.
3 | Use the public information officer for all community messages.
4 | Escalate to unified command when protective action distances exceed one mile.
3: UN 1005 | Ammonia, anhydrous | 125 | Toxic inhalation gas
UN 1977 | Nitrogen, refrigerated liquid | 120 | Cryogenic gas
UN 1993 | Flammable liquids, n.o.s. | 128 | Flammable liquid
4: Ammonia, anhydrous | Small | 100 ft | 0.2 mi | 0.6 mi
5: Hot Zone | Entry only for trained responders in assigned PPE.
Warm Zone | Decontamination corridor and exposure monitoring.
Cold Zone | Command, triage, media staging, and family assistance.
Public Perimeter | Community notification and traffic control.
6: Toxic inhalation gas | Positive-pressure SCBA | Level A or B | Continuous air monitoring
Flammable liquid | SCBA when vapor present | Structural turnout or Level B | Eliminate ignition sources
Cryogenic gas | Face shield and insulated gloves | Thermal protection | Ventilate low areas
7: Step 1 | Gross rinse before medical evaluation when contamination is visible.
Step 2 | Remove outer garments and bag as contaminated material.
Step 3 | Document exposed person name, time, substance, and symptoms.
Step 4 | Transfer clean patients to triage with exposure tag attached.
8: Release owner | Capstone Harbor Emergency Management
